market_context: Futures point higher (+0.53% S&P, +0.70% Nasdaq) despite Middle East tensions as U.S.-Iran ceasefire extended. Tesla reports Q1 earnings after close with mixed expectations. Oil prices up 0.79% on geopolitical uncertainty.

predictions.reasoning: Premarket futures show +0.53% gain as Trump extended Iran ceasefire, reducing immediate war risks. Strong earnings expectations with Q1 growth forecasted at 12.5% for S&P 500 companies should support upward momentum despite geopolitical overhang.

Tesla reports Q1 earnings after close with consensus EPS of $0.33-0.37 vs $0.50 last quarter. Company missed delivery targets by 7,600 units and has 50,000+ vehicle inventory buildup. Energy storage segment halved sequentially, creating multiple headwinds.

Marvell surged 6.9% premarket on reports of Google chip partnership talks for two new AI processors. Stock up 75% YTD with strong AI infrastructure demand. Recent Nvidia $2B investment adds credibility to AI transformation thesis.

UnitedHealth beat Q1 expectations with $111.7B revenue vs $109.66B consensus and raised 2026 EPS guidance above $18.25. Healthcare sector benefits from stable demand and the company showed strong operational execution in earnings call.

Nvidia faces competitive pressure from Marvell-Google chip partnership news and broader market rotation. Despite strong fundamentals, stock may see profit-taking as investors digest news that major cloud providers are developing alternative AI chip solutions.
